اسرائیل.e: Borrowed from Arabic إِسْرَائِيل (ʔisrāʔīl), itself from Hebrew יִשְׂרָאֵל (yisrá'él).

اسطوخودوس.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اسطوخودوس (ustūxūdūs), from Arabic أُسْطُوخُودُوس (ʔusṭūḵūdūs), from Ancient Greek στοιχάς (stoikhás).

اسطورہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اسطوره (ustūra) from Arabic أُسْطُورة (ʔusṭūra).

اسفنج.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْفَنْج (isfanj), borrowed from Arabic إِسْفَنْج (ʔisfanj), borrowed from Ancient Greek σπόγγος (spóngos).

senses.g: name of the son of Gishtāsb, of the first dynasty of Persian kings.
dropping or casting her young (an animal)
Scotland (a constituent country of the United Kingdom)
alternative form of اسکاٹ لینڈ (askāṭ lainḍ): Scotland (a constituent country of the United Kingdom)

اسلام.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْلَام (islām), borrowed from Arabic إِسْلَام (ʔislām), from أَسْلَمَ (ʔaslama).

senses.g: Islamabad (the capital city of Pakistan, located within the Islamabad Capital Territory)
former name of چٹگاؤں (caṭgāõ): Chittagong (a city in Bangladesh)
synonym of اننت ناگ: Anantnag (a city in the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India)
synonym of ٹلفورڈ (ṭilforḍ): Tilford (a village and civil parish south-east of Farnham, Waverley borough, Surrey, England (OS grid ref SU8743))
Islamic Republic of Pakistan (official name of Pakistan: a country in South Asia)
alternative form of اسلامی جمہوریہ پاکستان (islāmī jumhūriya-e-pākistān, “Islamic Republic of Pakistan”), spelling with ezafe/izāfat

اسلحہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسْلِحَه (asliha), from Arabic أَسْلِحَة (ʔasliḥa), plural of سِلَاح (silāḥ, “weapon”).

senses.g: high fashion, guise, manner, routine, style

اسم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْم (ism), borrowed from Arabic اِسْم (ism).

senses.g: a male given name from Arabic

اسماعیل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْمَاعِیل (ismā'īl), from Arabic إِسْمَاعِيل (ʔismāʕīl), from Hebrew יִשְׁמָעֵאל (yishma'él, yišmāʿēl).

اسیر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیر (asīr), from Arabic أَسِير (ʔasīr).
Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیر (asīr), from Arabic أَسِير (ʔasīr).

اسیری.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیرِی (asīrī). By surface analysis, اَسِیر (asīr) + ی (ī).

اشارات.e: From Arabic إِشَارَات (ʔišārāt), plural of إِشَارَة (ʔišāra).

senses.g: symbol, emblem (physical or otherwise, of any kind)

ex.u: (road transport) traffic signal

senses.g: ineffability, (something unable to be expressed through words).

اشارہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشَارَه (išāra), borrowed from Arabic إِشَارَة (ʔišāra), from أَشَارَ (ʔašāra). Sense 8 is a semantic loan from Punjabi اِشَارَہ (īśārah, “sign; indicator”).

senses.g: to denote, hint, imply, indicate, insinuate, mention, pinpoint, signify, tip, wink

اشاعت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشاعت (išā'at), taken from Arabic إشاعة (ʔišāʕa).

senses.g: Seville (the capital city of Andalusia, Spain)

اشتباہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشتباه (ištibāh), from Arabic verbal noun اِشْتِباه (ištibāh), from شَبَّهَ (šabbaha, “to assimilate”).

اشتباہی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشْتِبَاهِی (ištibāhī, “faulty, erroneous”).

senses.g: collaboration, participation, co-operation, affiliation (both in the narrow as well as composite senses)

ex.u: اُن دنوں تحریکِ عدمِ اشتراکِ عمل زوروں پر تھی۔
ex.r: mazāmīn-e-parem cand
ex.e: In those days the non-cooperation movement was in full swing.

senses.g: commonality or its associated ideologies such as socialism, communism, Marxism

ex.u: یَہاں تَک تو قَدِیم و جَدِید واقِعات میں تَشابُہ اَور اِشْتِراک ہَے۔
ex.r: yahā̃ tak to qadīm o jadīd vāqi'āt mẽ taśābu aur iśtirāk hai.
ex.e: To the extent that there is a resemblance and similarity in the ancient and modern events.

ex.u: ایک زَمانَہ تھا جَب اِنْسان میں خُصُوصی زَنانَہ اَور مَرْدانَہ اَعْضا دونوں ہوتے تھے ... نَباتات میں یہ اِشْتِراک اَبِھی تَک مَوجُود ہَے۔
ex.r: ek zamāna thā jab insān mẽ xusūsī zanāna aur mardāna a'zā donõ hote the ... nabātāt mẽ ye iśtirāk abhī tak maujūd hai.
ex.e: There was a time when humans had both specialised male and female organs ... In plants this combination still exists [today].

senses.g: partnership, sharing (usually in conjunction with فی (fī, “in/with”))

ex.u: وہ کسی فرقہ کا مذہبی پیشوا بننے کو اشتراک فی النبوة سمجھتے تھے۔
ex.r: hālāt-e-sar sayyad
ex.e: He understood religious leadership as an act of sharing in prophethood.

اشتراک.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشْتِرَاک (ištirāk), borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِرَاك (ištirāk), from اِشْتَرَكَ (ištaraka).

اشتراکیت.e: Borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِرَاكِيَّة (ištirākiyya). By surface analysis, اشتراک (iśtirāk, “participation”) + یت (iyyat, “-ism”).

اشتہا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشتها (ištihā), from Arabic اِشْتِهَاء (ištihāʔ).

اشتیاق.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشْتِیَاق (ištiyāq), from Arabic اِشْتِيَاق (ištiyāq).

اشر.e: From Sanskrit अश्रु (áśru), from Proto-Indo-European *dáḱru-. Cognate with Ancient Greek δάκρυ (dákru), Lithuanian ašara.

senses.g: the Book of Isaiah (one of the books of the Bible)
Isaiah (a prophet, the author of the Book of Isaiah)

اشفاق.e: From Classical Persian اشفاق (ašfāq, išfāq), from Arabic إِشْفَاق (ʔišfāq), verbal noun of أَشْفَقَ (ʔašfaqa), and أَشْفَاق (ʔašfāq), the plural of شَفَق (šafaq).

اشک.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشک (ašk) from Proto-Iranian *Hácru, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂éḱru (“tear”). Doublet of آن٘سُو (ā̃sū) and اشر (aśru).

senses.g: Ashoka (an emperor of the Mauryan Empire)
a male given name, Ashok, from Sanskrit, of Hindu usage

ex.u: گھوڑے کو اصطبل میں لےکر کھانا دو۔
ex.r: ghoṛe ko astabal mẽ lekar khānā do.
ex.e: Take the horse to the stable and give it some food.

اصطبل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اصطبل (istabl), from Arabic إِصْطَبْل (ʔiṣṭabl), via Byzantine Greek στάβλον (stáblon) from Latin stabulum.

اصطلاح.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِصْطِلَاح (istilāh), from Arabic اِصْطِلَاح (iṣṭilāḥ).

senses.g: Isfahan (a city in Iran, the seat of Isfahan County's Central District and the capital of Isfahan Province, the third largest city of Iran; the former capital of Iran, from 1598–1736)
a male given name from Persian

اصفہان.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِصْفَهَان (isfahān), ultimately from Old Persian *spādānaʰ (“army, adj.”) or *spādānām (“of the armies”), from 𐎿𐎱𐎠𐎭 (spādaʰ, “army”).

اصل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْل (asl), borrowed from Arabic أَصْل (ʔaṣl).

senses.g: hypostasis, germ, lineage, quid, root, rudiment

اصل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْل (asl), borrowed from Arabic أَصْل (ʔaṣl).

اصلا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اصلاً (aslan), from Arabic أَصْلًا (ʔaṣlan).

senses.g: improvement, atonement, correction, emendation, modification, reform
authentic, intrinsic, primal, primary, primeval, primordial, radical, ultimate, unadulterated, unfeigned, veritable, virtual

اصلی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْلِی (aslī), borrowed from Arabic أَصْلِيّ (ʔaṣliyy), from أَصْل (ʔaṣl) + ـِيّ (-iyy).
Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْلِی (aslī), borrowed from Arabic أَصْلِيّ (ʔaṣliyy), from أَصْل (ʔaṣl) + ـِيّ (-iyy).

اصلیت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْلِیَّت (asliyyat), from Arabic أَصْلِيَّة (ʔaṣliyya), equivalent to اَصْل (asl) + ـیَت (-iyat).

اصول.e: From Arabic أُصُول (ʔuṣūl), plural of Arabic أَصْل (ʔaṣl).

اصولی.e: Derived from Arabic أُصُولِيّ (ʔuṣūliyy). By surface analysis, اصول (“principles”) + ی (ī).

senses.g: plural of صِہْر (sihr); in-laws (relations by marriage)

ex.u: اَصْہَار الْغَالِب

اصہار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَصْهَار (ashār), from Arabic أَصْهَار (ʔaṣhār).

اضافت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِضَافَت (izāfat), from Arabic إِضَافَة (ʔiḍāfa).

اضافہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِضَافَه (izāfa), borrowed from Arabic إِضَافَة (ʔiḍāfa), from أَضَافَ (ʔaḍāfa).

senses.g: anxiety, perturbation, restlessness, turmoil, uneasiness, worriment

اضطراب.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِضْطِرَاب (iztirāb), from Arabic اِضْطِرَاب (iḍṭirāb, “disturbance; disorder”).

اطاعت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِطَاعَت (itā'at), borrowed from Arabic إِطَاعَة (ʔiṭāʕa), from أَطَاعَ (ʔaṭāʕa). First attested in c. 1500 as Middle Hindi اِطاعَت (at̤a't /⁠itā'at⁠/).

ex.u: اللہ اور اُس کے رسول کی اطاعت کرو!
ex.r: Allāh aur us ke rasūl ki itā’at karo!
ex.e: Obey Allāh and His messengers!

اطراف.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَطْرَاف (atrāf), from Arabic أَطْرَاف (ʔaṭrāf).

ex.u: اطلاع ملنا
ex.r: ittelā milnā
ex.e: to receive a notice/information

اطلاع.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِطِّلَاع (ittilā'), borrowed from Arabic اِطِّلَاع (iṭṭilāʕ), from اِطَّلَعَ (iṭṭalaʕa).

اطلس.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اطلس (atlas), from Arabic أَطْلَس (ʔaṭlas).

اطمینان.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِطْمِینَان (itmīnān), from Arabic اِطْمِئْنَان (iṭmiʔnān).

senses.g: indication, declaration, disclosure, manifestation, proclamation, revelation, statement, testimony, vent

اظہار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِظْهَار (izhār), from Arabic إِظْهَار (ʔiẓhār).

اعتبار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِبَار (i'tibār), borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِبَار (iʕtibār), from اِعْتَبَرَ (iʕtabara).

اعتدال.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِدَال (i'tidāl), borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِدَال (iʕtidāl), from اِعْتَدَلَ (iʕtadala).

اعتراض.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِرَاض (i'tirāz), borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِرَاض (iʕtirāḍ), from اِعْتَرَضَ (iʕtaraḍa).

اعتماد.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِمَاد (i'timād), from Arabic اِعْتِمَاد (iʕtimād).
Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِمَاد (i'timād), from Arabic اِعْتِمَاد (iʕtimād).

اعلی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَعْلیٰ (a'lā), from Arabic أَعْلَى (ʔaʕlā), elative degree of عَالٍ (ʕālin) and عَلِيّ (ʕaliyy).

ex.u: ارے میری بیٹی اغوا ہو گئی!
ex.r: are merī beṭī iġvā ho gaī!
ex.e: Oh no, my daughter has been kidnapped!

اغوا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اغوا (iġwā), from Arabic إِغْوَاء (ʔiḡwāʔ), from أَغْوَى (ʔaḡwā, “to lure, to tempt”).

افراد.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian افراد (afrād), plural form of فرد (fard), from Arabic فَرْد (fard, “individual”).

senses.g: Africa (the continent south of Europe and between the Atlantic and Indian Oceans)
Africa (a former province of the Roman Empire, containing what is now Tunisia and portions of coastal Algeria and Libya)

ex.u: اَفْزائِشِ حُسْن
ex.r: afzāiś-i husn
ex.e: beautification

ex.u: اَفْزائِشِ نَسْل
ex.r: afzāiś-i nasl
ex.e: breeding

افسانہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَفْسَانَه (afsāna). Doublet of فَسانَہ (fasāna).

senses.g: inflection of اَفْسانَہ (afsāna, “tale; legend”): — direct plural
inflection of اَفْسانَہ (afsāna, “tale; legend”): — oblique/vocative singular

ex.u: اُس کو کچھ افسوس نہیں ہوا۔
ex.r: usko kuch afsos nahī̃ huā.
ex.e: He felt no remorse.

senses.g: Afzal Gunj (a neighbourhood of Hyderabad, Telangana, India)

افضل گنج.e: Named after Afzal-ud-Daulah, from اَفْضَل (afzal) + ـگَنْج (-ganj).

افطار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian افطار (iftār), from Arabic إِفْطَار (ʔifṭār).

senses.g: Afghanistan (a landlocked country between Central Asia and South Asia)

افق.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian افق (ufuq), from Arabic أُفُق (ʔufuq).

افلاطون.e: Borrowed from Arabic أَفْلَاطُون (ʔaflāṭūn), from Ancient Greek Πλάτων (Plátōn).

senses.g: very clever or wise person; overclever or cocky person

افلاطون.e: Borrowed from Arabic أَفْلَاطُون (ʔaflāṭūn), from Ancient Greek Πλάτων (Plátōn).

افواہ.e: Borrowed from Arabic أَفْوَاه (ʔafwāh), plural of فَم (fam, “mouth”).

افیون.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَفْیُون (afyūn), from Arabic أَفْيُون (ʔafyūn), ultimately from Ancient Greek ὄπιον (ópion).

senses.g: second to last call to prayer

اقامت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِقَامَت (iqāmat), from Arabic إِقَامَة (ʔiqāma).

اقتدار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اقتدار (iqtidār), from Arabic اِقْتِدَار (iqtidār).

اقتصادی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِقْتِصَادِی (iqtisādī), from Arabic اِقْتِصَادِيّ (iqtiṣādiyy). By surface analysis, اقتصاد (iqtisād) + ـی (-ī).

اقرار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اقرار (iqrār), ultimately from Arabic إِقْرَار (ʔiqrār).

اقلیت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اقلیت (aqalliyyat), from Ottoman Turkish اقلیت (ekalliyet). First attested in c. 1936.

اقلیم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِقْلِیم (iqlīm), from Arabic إِقْلِيم (ʔiqlīm), from Ancient Greek κλίμα (klíma).

اقوام.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اقوام (aqvām), from Arabic أَقْوَام (ʔaqwām).

اکا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓 (ekka), from Sanskrit एक (eka).
Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓 (ekka), from Sanskrit एक (eka).

Borrowed from Dravidian, or inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸 (akkā), from Sanskrit अक्का (akkā), ultimately from Proto-Dravidian *akka. Compare Kannada ಅಕ್ಕ (akka), Malayalam അക്ക (akka), Telugu అక్క (akka), Tamil அக்கா (akkā), and Konkani and Marathi अक्का (akkā).

اکادمی.e: Assimilated from English academy, from French académie, from Latin acadēmīa, from Ancient Greek Ἀκαδημία (Akadēmía).

senses.g: a bad season, a season of scarcity

ex.u: دیس میں بہت بھیانک اکال پڑا ہے۔
ex.r: des mẽ bahut bhayānak akāl paṛā hai.
ex.e: There is a terrible famine happening in the nation.

senses.g: an administrative unit of a country

اکتوبر.e: Borrowed from Portuguese outubro, influenced by English October. Compare Classical Persian اَکْتُوبَر (aktūbar), Konkani ओतुब्र (otubra), Marathi आक्तोबर (āktobar), all of which could be possible intermediaries.

اکٹھا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀏𑀕𑀢𑁆𑀣 (egattha), from Sanskrit ए॒क॒स्थ (ekasthá), from ए॒क (eká, “one”) + स्थ (stha, “standing”)..

ex.u: ہَم اَکْثَر اِس مَحَلّے میں آیا کَرتے تھے۔
ex.r: ham aksar is mahalle mẽ āyā karte the.
ex.e: We used to come to this neighbourhood often.

ex.u: اَکْثَر جو ہَم کَہْتے ہَیں وہ کَرْتے نَہِیں۔
ex.r: aksar jo ham kahte ha͠i vo karte nahī̃.
ex.e: We do not do what we usually say.

اکثر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَکْثَر (aksar), borrowed from Arabic أَكْثَر (ʔakṯar).

اکثریت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَکْثَرِیَّت (aksariyyat), from Arabic أَكْثَرِيَّة (ʔakṯariyya).

senses.g: verbal noun of اَکَڑْنا (akaṛnā) — stiffness (state of being stiff; tightness)
verbal noun of اَکَڑْنا (akaṛnā) — crookedness, twist, bend
conceit, arrogance, vanity (excessive self-esteem; disregard for the opinions of others)

ex.u: اکڑ سے بات کرنا
ex.r: akaṛ se bāt karnā
ex.e: to speak conceitedly

اکڑ.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi اکڙ (akṛ /⁠akaṛ⁠/) (c. 1679), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀆𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀟 (*ākkaḍa). Cognates Cognate with Punjabi آکَڑ / ਆਕੜ (ākaṛ), Pahari-Potwari آکَڑ (ākaṛ) and Marathi अकड (akaḍ).

ex.u: اِس شبد سے کچھ اکشر مٹ گئے ہیں
ex.r: is śabd se kuch akṣar miṭ gae ha͠i
ex.e: Some letters have been erased from this word.

ex.u: اِس شبد کو اور اکشر جوڑو
ex.r: is śabd ko aur akṣar joṛo
ex.e: Add more letters/syllables to this word.

senses.g: sole; only (concerning progeny; where the possibility of more than one is conceivable)

ex.u: یہ نوجوان جسکی ۲۱؍ برس کی عمر ہو گی اکلوتا بیٹا اُس دولتمند کا تھا
ex.r: mirzā sāhib hairat dihalvī
ex.e: This young man, who's probably 21’ years old, was the only son of that rich man

senses.g: single, lone (one who does not have a partner)

ex.u: مگر میرے نزدیک میرا اکلوتا بھائ شوکت بھی ہے
ex.r: muhammad sarvar
ex.e: However, I have a single brother [who is called] Shaukat

اکلوتا.e: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀮𑀉𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*ĕkkalaütta), from Sanskrit एकल-पुत्र (ekala-putra, “only son”). Compare Punjabi اکلوتا (iklauttā). Related to اِکل (ikal).

senses.g: inflection of اِکْلَوتا (iklautā, “only; sole”): — direct masculine plural
inflection of اِکْلَوتا (iklautā, “only; sole”): — oblique masculine singular/plural
now, at this moment, at present

اکیس.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀯𑀻𑀲𑀻 (ekkavīsī), from Sanskrit एकविंशति (ékaviṃśati).

ex.u: وہ اکیلا ہے۔
ex.r: vo akelā hai.
ex.e: He is alone.

اکیلا.e: Inherited from Old Hindi अकेला (akelā), from Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀮𑀬 (ĕkkalaya), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*ekkalla) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-) (compare Sanskrit एकल (ekala)), probably from Sanskrit एक (éka, “one”).

ex.u: وہ گھر اکیلے جائےگا۔
ex.r: vō ghar akele jāegā
ex.e: He will go home by himself.

senses.g: inflection of اکیلا (akelā, “alone”): — oblique/vocative masculine singular
inflection of اکیلا (akelā, “alone”): — direct/oblique/vocative masculine plural

ex.u: پہلوان اکھاڑے میں کشتی کر رہے ہے۔
ex.r: pahalvān akhāṛe mẽ kuśtī kar rahe hai.
ex.e: The wrestlers are fighting in the arena.

اکھاڑا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit अक्षवाट (akṣavāṭa, “wrestling-ground”).

اکھروٹ.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀓𑁆𑀔𑁄𑀝 (akkhoṭa), from Sanskrit अक्षोट (akṣoṭa).
